name: Angel Food Summer Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, mix together berries, sugar, liqueur, and lemon peel.
Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until berries are soft and sugar has dissolved, 5 to 8 minutes. Do not overcook. Let cool.
2. Trim the cake of any browned edges using a serrated knife. Cut into half-inch slices, then cut slices into triangles.
3. Line the bottom and sides of a 1 1/2 to 2 quart bowl or souffle dish with three-quarters of the cake. Fill gaps with small pieces if necessary.
4. Spoon filling into the lined bowl. Top with remaining cake triangles, covering berries completely.
5. Place a plate slightly smaller than the bowl's diameter on top of the pudding. Weigh down the saucer with a 4 or 5 pound can. Chill for 8 hours or overnight.
6. Remove the plate and can. Carefully unmold pudding onto a serving plate.
7. In a large bowl, beat cream at medium speed until soft peaks form. Beat in confectioner's sugar.
8. Pipe small mounds of cream mixture on top of pudding using a pastry bag fitted with a large star tip. Garnish with fresh berries and sprigs of mint.
